ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-02-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Valencia for and with Christine Brown.

The Court expressed concern with the number of purchases/sales of the stocks. Mr. Valencia stated that the reason for diversity of the accounts was to maximize return to provide for the Beneficiaries in the future, that the stock market crashed and that his Client relies on the advice of Morgan Stanley. In addition, Counsel stated that they now have five securities, that the Trusts have been consolidated and that the current value is $190,000.00. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #8, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-14-2010 Continued To Jul-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jun-08-2010 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #7, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Valencia for and with Christine Brown.

Mr. Valencia stated that Ms. Brown was in constant contact with the Investment Advisors from Morgan Stanley and that this was a complicated matter that required Ms. Brown to spend a lot of time with and handle matters for Violet Hicks. In addition, he stated that the issue regarding the checks written by Ms. Hicks had occurred one time only and that measures were being taken to prevent that from happening in the future. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
